title: Rename Gemma2 node to Gemma in AI Engineer roadmap
author: DorukTaneli
body:
## Suggested change

Rename the `Gemma2` node in the AI Engineer roadmap to `Gemma`.

## Reason

The other model nodes use model-family names without version numbers. `Gemma2` is the only version-specific label, while `Gemma` represents the current model family and avoids tying the roadmap to an outdated release.

The related content update will also rename the sidebar title and remove the outdated "Gemma explained: What's new in Gemma 2" resource.

Would you like me to create a pr for this?

title: This Repository is No Longer 6th Most Starred Repo on Github
author: gdaffa
body:
### Detailed Description

Unfortunately, this repo is not 6th most starred repo. If you see [this](https://github.com/search?o=desc&q=stars%3A%3E100000&s=stars&type=Repositories), the link that provided by `Join the Community` section in the footer of all roadmap, the title has been taken by [openclaw](https://github.com). But the good news is this repo is still on top 10, its 7th most starred repo to be precise.

Should the footer be changed to "7th most starred repository on Github", "Once the 6th most starred repository on Github", or something else?

title: Lesson progress counter resets per module instead of showing cumulative count across course
author: fhraj-0
body:
### What is the URL where the issue is happening

https://roadmap.sh/ai/library/courses

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

### **Bug:**
Course progress indicator (e.g. "X/Y lessons") shows incorrect cumulative count when navigating between modules.
Steps to reproduce:

Open a course with multiple modules (example: course with 7 modules, 35 total lessons)
Go to Module 1, Lesson 4 → progress shows 4/35 ✅ (correct)
Move to Module 2, Lesson 1 → progress shows 1/35 ❌ (incorrect)

### **Expected behavior:**
Progress should show cumulative lesson count across all modules. If Module 1 has 7 lessons, then Module 2, Lesson 1 should show 8/35, not 1/35.
Actual behavior:
Counter resets to per-module lesson number instead of continuing the cumulative count.
Additional context:

Total modules: 7
Total lessons: 35
Module 1 has 7 lessons (confirmed 4/35 shown correctly at lesson 4)
Bug appears when crossing into next module

### Output from browser console (if any)

_No response_

### Participation

- [x] I am willing to submit a pull request for this issue.

title: If come to language, there is not only Python!
author: dominexmacedon-docs
body:
### Detailed Description

## A new interpreter or language built on the top of Node.js

That's **Lopo** Programming language. This is just for **announcement**. 
**Lopo** provides core features such as variable, operators, strings, array, function, entity, module import and other critical features.

**Conditional logic** is one of the most challenging part of a language but it was successfully implemented in **Lopo**. Nested structures are fully supported and resolved. 

Try it out at [Lopo Programming Language](https://github.com/dominexmacedon-docs/lopo-docs). Thanks

title: Suggestion: Add type annotations and docstrings/comments to python roadmap
author: nerrader
body:
### Roadmap URL

https://roadmap.sh/python

### Suggestions

I think type annotations/type hinting and comments/docstrings should be explicitly shown in the roadmap somewhere.

Type annotations are pretty much crucial in most codebases due to the addition of static type checkers, which catches errors before they even happen, and it's a good habit for a beginner to start using type annotations as soon as possible.

Comments and docstrings should be taught (as well as how to use them effectively) as they can like you know improve readability of code. I think its just a fundamental thing that should be taught here. Maybe you can also make a reference to the autoDocstring extension in VSCode as a way to help make better docstrings.

Maybe you could also add an introduction to PEP8 guidelines as well somewhere, but I don't think its that critical compared to the other two suggestions here.

title: Bug: DevOps Skills node loads Introduction content on Forward Deployed Engineer roadmap
author: swdev-Animesh
body:
### What is the URL where the issue is happening

https://roadmap.sh/forward-deployed-engineer

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### Describe the Bug

### Bug Description
On the Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) roadmap, clicking the **DevOps Skills** node loads the content markdown for the **Introduction** node instead of DevOps resources.

### Root Cause Analysis
Inside the `src/data/roadmaps/forward-deployed-engineer/content/` directory, there is a duplicate node ID conflict causing a build-time overwrite condition. 

Here is how the files are currently structured in the workspace:

| File Name | Content Inside File | Associated Node ID | Status |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `introduction@PBtnaTE_5AMOa5mxbhoT2.md` | Introduction Text | `PBtnaTE_5AMOa5mxbhoT2` | **Correct** (Official Intro) |
| `devops-skills@LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj.md` | DevOps Resources | `LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj` | **Correct** (Official DevOps) |
| `introduction@LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj.md` | Introduction Text | `LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj` |  **Ghost File (Duplicate ID)** |

Because the accidental ghost file `introduction@LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj.md` shares the exact same node ID (`LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj`) as the DevOps-skills file, the build script overwrites the content with the introduction text when the DevOps node is requested.

### Proposed Fix
Remove the accidental ghost file `introduction@LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj.md`. This leaves the `LRBwP7EQj4byox7yKdPAj` ID uniquely mapped to the DevOps Skills content, resolving the conflict.

### Output from browser console (if any)

_No response_

### Participation

- [x] I am willing to submit a pull request for this issue.
